title: [Epidemiology of bone and joint disease - the present and future - . Genetic epidemiology on osteoarthritis].
abstract: Although a large number of studies have been made to identify genetic loci associated with osteoarthritis (OA), there are only a few that have been reported to be associated with OA in many regions and countries. One of the reasons for the lack of success may be small effect sizes. A sufficiently large sample size is required for the genetic epidemiology on OA to elucidate genetic influence regarding the occurrence and progression.
